title: Dietary Antioxidants Prevent Age-Related Retinal Pigment Epithelium Damage and Blindness in Mice Lacking the Alpha-v-Beta-5 Integrin. In an animal model of vision loss representative of human macular degeneration, a grape-enriched diet significantly decreased 4-hydroxynonenal-adduct content and lipofuscin granule buildup and prevented age-related cone and rod photoreceptor dysfunction. The grape diet was protective when fed at a younger age, 3 to 6 months or 6 to 9 months, but offered no protection when fed at 9 to 12 months. The grape powder was significantly more protective than lutein.

sentences.text: Dietary Antioxidants Prevent Age-Related Retinal Pigment Epithelium Damage and Blindness in Mice Lacking the Alpha-v-Beta-5 Integrin.

In an animal model of vision loss representative of human macular degeneration, a grape-enriched diet significantly decreased 4-hydroxynonenal-adduct content and lipofuscin granule buildup and prevented age-related cone and rod photoreceptor dysfunction.
The grape diet was protective when fed at a younger age, 3 to 6 months or 6 to 9 months, but offered no protection when fed at 9 to 12 months.
The grape powder was significantly more protective than lutein.
